Extracurricular Activities Are Important Extracurricular activities are important because they allow children to develop valuable life-skills such as: teamwork; communication skills; leadership abilities; time-management skills; conflict-resolution skills; self-esteem; confidence-building skills; goal-setting skills; etc.  These skills can help prepare them for adulthood by allowing them to: find jobs more easily after graduation; get promoted at their job faster than others who do not possess these skills; make better decisions when faced with difficult situations (e.g., choosing which college program is best suited for them; deciding whether or not it would be wise to marry someone); live happier lives overall once they become adults.   For example,... Continue reading... Extracurricular activities also give kids opportunities to meet people from all walks of life--people whose backgrounds may differ greatly from their own--and learn about other cultures through first-hand experience instead of just hearing about those differences second-hand from teachers and/or parents/guardians.  In addition, participating in extra-curricular activities gives kids chances to try out things they've never tried before without fearing failure since there will always be another opportunity to try again if something doesn't go right the first time around.  
Finally, participation in extra-curricular activities allows kids to discover what they're truly passionate about so they'll know exactly where they'd like to focus during high school and beyond....

"Noi, schiavi di bomber e bidoni" è un articolo che esamina l'impatto dei cambiamenti climatici sull'umanità. L'articolo inizia sottolineando come il clima stia diventando sempre più estremo a causa dell'aumento delle emissioni di gas serra nell'atmosfera. Ciò sta portando ad eventi meteorologici più frequenti ed intensi, tra cui siccità, alluvioni, uragani e ondate di calore. Questi disastri naturali sono particolarmente dannosi per coloro che vivono nei paesi meno sviluppati del mondo, poiché non hanno accesso alle risorse necessarie per proteggersi o riprendersi dagli effetti devastanti degli eventi meteorologici estremi. L'articolo poi passa a discutere gli sforzi attuali per ridurre le emissioni globali di gas serra e mitigare i rischi associati ai cambiamenti climatici. Sottolinea l'importanza della transizione verso fonti di energia rinnovabile, come solare, eolica e idroelettrica, al fine di sostituire la nostra dipendenza dai combustibili fossili. Inoltre, sottolinea l'urgenza nel raggiungimento di una maggiore efficienza energetica attraverso tecnologie avanzate e pratiche migliori. Infine, l'articolo incoraggia individui, aziende e governi a lavorare insieme per trovare modi innovativi per contribuire alla lotta contro il cambiamento climatico

Many students tend to forget things they learned at school, in the summer. During summer, teenagers are out having fun and taking a break from school. Although the school year is over, students should not lose focus on what is important. Since teens need to keep focus, a summer project would be the perfect task for them.  Summer projects should be designed by their up coming teacher students will get familiarized with what they will learn  teachers will assign projects that will be beneficial to their students, teachers will have a criteria showing students what to look for. going into a new school year, no one really knows what the year will be like. Having a summer project allows students to have some knowledge on what the new year might bring. if Generic_School made students do one summer project it would not only help their grade for the first quarter, but also show them know what they will learn. Although there will be students who will not do the projects, there will also be people who will complete them because they are serious and focused on school. Students who work hard and want to be successful in life will see that summer projects given to them will be beneficial to them.  teachers know what is best for their students.  A prime example here at Generic_School is a geometry teacher, although she assigns a handful of work, she knows that if the students completes it, it will be extremely helpful to them. Most of time students will complain about the amount of work a teacher might give them but in the long run it will help them tremendously. Teachers giving out projects to do in the summer may sound dreadful but it will, undoubtedly help them. To add on teachers will always tell students what to look for and give them a rubric to help them. teachers will never assign a student a project and not give them a rubric. Often times, students will argue and pout about a project, but when a teacher gives their students a rubric and the criteria of the project, it makes it twice as much easier. Students who are serious about school and want to bring up their GPA will not complain about one measly summer project. Also, summer projects will never be too hard, since the school year has not yet started  Teachers will most likely tell their students what to do and how to do it. Even though the projects assigned to students may be easy, some may complain about the subject and want to do a project on a subject they choose.

While some students may prefer to do a summer project on what they want. The pros to this would be that they are actually interested in the subject and more motivated about it. They would also, most likely get a better grade on something they are genuinely concerned about. For example, if a student loved the ocean, they would be more excited doing a project on marine life or how unique the ocean is than doing a project about different rocks. Although this may be true, teacher-designed projects would be more beneficial than a student choosing their own subject.  teacher-designed projects would be more productive for students than student-designed projects. It gives students the opportunity to learn a little before the school year starts. Teachers will assign students projects that will be helpful to them. Also, the projects would not be extremely hard because the teacher is essentially telling students how to complete the project. Schools should allow teachers to assign the students projects to do in the summer because it will help them a great deal more than students choosing their own projects.

The electoral college has many flaws, which include but are not limited to the potential electors becoming too attached to a candidate, the general public not understanding the process, and debates not being effective in informing the electorate. As Richard A. Posner argues, the electoral college is the "least bad" option and so we must accept the flaws and rely on the quirks of the system. The electoral college does a poor job of choosing the president because of the following reasons: the likelihood of candidate complacency, voter ignorance, voter rage if the candidate doesn't win, polarization of voters, and candidates not understanding the process. These problems are not new, however, the increasing divisiveness of politics over the past two centuries indicates that these problems will only continue. Some examples of the drawbacks of the electoral college include candidate complacency, which can happen when a candidate has a strong lead and is virtually guaranteed victory. Since the electoral college was created, there is not a single election that the winner had a 0% chance of winning, however, some candidates show less support once they win. Candidate complacency could lead to bad policies as the elected official is too scared to take any risks or offend anyone. Ignorance about the election is common, although the chances of a voter knowing about every election are high, the chances of them caring enough to learn about every election is low. There is also voter rage if the preferred candidate doesn't win. The implications are that there could be riots in the streets or other chaotic consequences if the candidate does not win. Polarization of voters is another consequence of the electoral college, and while it is true that we do not choose our presidents through a national election, it is a side-effect of the fact that we only vote for one person. Candidates might not want to run against fear of being branded unelectable. Lastly, the issue of candidates not understanding the process. Candidates only need a small number of votes to win the electoral college vote. There are 670 million Americans, and many people only need 20 votes. If a candidate has a large fraction of the popular vote but the total number of electoral college votes, this does not guarantee victory. A good example is Ross Perot in 1992. He received 19.5% of the popular vote but won 23.9% of the electoral college votes. He could have lost the election but because of the nature of the system, he did not. The electoral college system needs to be replaced. A popular vote would not create dynastic families, since the most popular candidate will not always become President. There are too many unknown variables that are difficult to account for. For example, who would hold the national primaries, would they be mandatory? Could candidates finagle their way onto the election for fun and profit, which would inflate the budgets of candidates? What about Independent candidates? Another advantage of a popular vote is that it gives the power back to the people. The power of the electoral college is that it creates a false sense of security for the candidate. The candidate believes they have enough electoral college votes because past presidents have. However, the electoral college ignores voter id, which brings me to my next point, voter rage if the candidate doesn't win. The candidate has spent a lot of time/energy trying to get the popular vote, and when they fail, it's always perceived that the candidate has "screwed the pooch". This is not a recipe for a healthy democracy. Lastly, the debates do not provide voters with a sufficient amount of information to make an informed decision. Most people do not know what constitutional provisions are applicable to current issues, which is probably why the United States has never had a primary election. There are many advantages of a popular vote, such as preventing dynastic families, not requiring a national primary, and simplifying the campaign process. Another advantage is that it will encourage voting, which is important for two reasons. The first is to enhance the democracy and eliminates voter apathy. The candidate who gets a majority of the popular vote would win the election. The other reason is financial. The popular vote will cost money because it creates a requirement for fundraising. As a student in the 21st century, I feel we need to change the electoral college. The candidates do not know what the issues will be, the cost of campaigns increases, and voter apathy and ignorance remain issues. Also, I don't think we can turn back time and undo the shortcomings of the electoral college. Therefore, the electoral college is indefensible.

Currently, the automobile industry is investigating methods to allow cars to drive themselves with only human assistance. These technologies could potentially save time and money for individuals. In addition to reduced traffic collisions, driverless cars could also reduce the stress caused by having to sit in traffic. For example, if you are in a rush and stuck in traffic, you could potentially download a TV show or audio book to your device. After arriving at your destination, you could hand off driving duties to the car software, allowing you to arrive at your destination much faster than if you were manually driving. Even if you are not stuck in traffic, human drivers currently waste a lot of time sitting in traffic. Given that time is Money, this wasted time could also cost individuals money. With driverless cars, there would be no need for a human driver to sit in the driver's seat. This could free up the passenger seat, allowing passengers to do other things, such as read or connect with friends on social media. Despite these potential benefits, there are a number of concerns that should be considered before adopting driverless cars. First, a key risk with driverless cars is the potential risk of hackers interfering with the vehicles' programming. For this reason, all cars would likely need to have some security or protection from malicious users. It is possible that some hackers might see this as an opportunity to disrupt the computers in vehicles, intentionally causing accidents. Another risk is that some cultures might object to the removal of the driver from the car. For example, take the traditional Japanese matcha consumption scenario. Matcha is best drunk while getting blindfolded, and it is expected that the host (typically a female) will be driving the car. This is because the Japanese tea ceremony was originally conceived as a way to help guide spiritual energy into the driver. This tea ceremony is likely to become obsolete once matcha-drinking drivers become a thing of the past.

In today's modern society, cars have become a staple in our daily lives. We rely on them for transportation, convenience, and comfort. However, there are several benefits to limiting the usage of cars in our daily lives. By doing so, we can help mitigate the detrimental effects of car emissions on the environment and the health of individuals, reduce stress levels and improve overall well-being, and even reap economic benefits.

One of the most significant impacts of car usage is the emission of harmful pollutants into the atmosphere. These emissions are responsible for a multitude of environmental issues, including climate change and air pollution. According to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), transportation accounts for the largest share of greenhouse gas emissions in the United States. By limiting the usage of cars, we can significantly reduce these emissions and contribute to a healthier and more sustainable environment.

Car emissions not only affect the environment but also have a direct impact on human health. The World Health Organization estimates that outdoor air pollution, largely caused by car emissions, is responsible for the premature deaths of millions of people worldwide. Additionally, car emissions contribute to respiratory problems, cardiovascular disease, and even cancer. By reducing the usage of cars, we can protect ourselves and future generations from these health risks.

Furthermore, embracing a no-car lifestyle can have positive effects on stress levels and overall well-being. Traffic congestion is a leading cause of stress and frustration for many individuals. By opting for alternative modes of transportation such as walking, biking, or public transportation, we can avoid the stress of traffic, enjoy physical activity, and improve our mental well-being.

Lastly, there are significant economic benefits to be gained from limiting car usage in cities and communities. The cost of car ownership, including fuel, insurance, maintenance, and parking, can be a significant drain on individuals' finances. In contrast, public transportation, walking, or biking can be much more cost-effective and improve the overall affordability of living in cities.

In conclusion, limiting the usage of cars in daily life has numerous benefits. It helps protect the environment, safeguard human health, reduce stress levels, and improve overall well-being. Embracing a no-car lifestyle also brings about economic advantages for both individuals and communities. In order to create a more sustainable and healthier future, it is crucial that we reconsider our reliance on cars and explore alternative modes of transportation.

E' arrivata nelle concessionarie Peugeot la nuova Peugeot RCZ-R, mostrata al pubblico per la prima volta nel luglio scorso. Il suo quattro cilindri di 1.598 cc a iniezione diretta di benzina, sovralimentato mediante turbocompressore twin-scroll, è cresciuto incredibilmente di potenza , passando da 200 a 270 cv. Il propulsore è stato sviluppato congiuntamente da BMW e Psa (il gruppo proprietario dei marchi Peugeot e Citroen) e infatti la sua caratteristica principale è l’alzata variabile delle valvole d’aspirazione oltre che il variatore di fase degli alberi a camme, stessa tecnologia utilizzata dalla Mini John Cooper Works GP (che però si ferma a 218 cv- approndisci qui ). Anche i livelli di coppia, rispetto alla versione precedente, sono cresciuti sensibilmente, passando da 28,0 a 33,7 kgm. Il peso dichiarato è di 1.280 kg, cioè 17 kg meno della RCZ 1.6 THP da 200 cv, ma soprattutto 30 kg più leggera della Porsche Cayman 2.7 (accreditata di 275 cv) - leggi qui la nostra prova della versione 3.4 - e 115 kg rispetto all’ Audi TT 2.0 TFSI quattro (272)

The Face is one of many landmarks found across Mars' surface. It has been studied since its discovery during NASA's Viking mission to the Red Planet back in 1976.
Scientists have debated whether or not this feature could be attributed to extraterrestrial life forms ever since they first saw photos taken from orbiting spacecrafts showing what appeared to be human-like facial features protruding out of rocky terrain near the planet's equator.  Some scientists believe these structures were formed naturally over time through geological processes such as erosion caused by windstorms while others argue that there are too many similarities between them and those seen elsewhere around our solar system (such as Earth) where no natural explanation can account for their existence. Il re del porno è stato preso in custodia dopo essere stato accusato di evasione fiscale. David Tassillo, CEO della popolare piattaforma per adulti YouPorn, ha affrontato le accuse secondo cui avrebbe nascosto milioni di dollari dalle autorità fiscali degli Stati Uniti. Tassillo si trovava a New York quando gli agenti dell'FBI lo hanno arrestato con l'accusa di aver utilizzato conti bancari offshore e società fantasma per nascondere i suoi guadagni dall'Internal Revenue Service (IRS). Secondo il Dipartimento di Giustizia degli Stati Uniti, Tassillo sarebbe riuscito a nascondere oltre 10 milioni di dollari dal governo federale tra il 2014 e il 2017. L'arresto di Tassillo segue un'indagine approfondita da parte delle forze dell'ordine statunitensi sulle sue attività finanziarie. Gli investigatori sospettavano che fosse coinvolto nell'evasione fiscale sin dalla sua nomina come amministratore delegato di YouPorn nel 2013. Nel corso dei loro lavori di indagine, sono venuti alla luce prove che suggerivano che Tassillo stesse trasferendo denaro all'estero al fine di eludere la tassazione statunitense

Recentemente è stato rilasciato un documentario intitolato "Leaving Neverland", che esamina le accuse di abuso su minori mosse contro il defunto cantante Michael Jackson. Il film ha causato molte polemiche, con alcuni accusando i registi del documentario di essere ingiustamente critici nei confronti dell'icona della musica pop e altri sostenendo che la verità deve venire alla luce indipendentemente dalle conseguenze per l'eredità di Jackson. Il documentario si concentra sulle storie di due uomini, Wade Robson e James Safechuck, entrambi ora adulti, che affermano di aver subito abusi da parte di Jackson quando erano bambini. Entrambi gli uomini hanno testimoniato in tribunale a favore di Jackson durante il processo penale del 2005 per presunti abusi sessuali su minorenni, ma ora sostengono di essersi pentiti delle loro testimonianze originali e di voler raccontare la vera storia dei fatti. I dettagli forniti nel documentario sono spesso scioccanti ed emozionanti, portando molti telespettatori ad interrogarsi sulla reputazione postuma di Jackson

Wie nun bekanntgegeben wurde, scheint es so, dass der ORF für die Übertragungsrechte der Fußball-Champions-League keine überhöhten Preise gezahlt hat. Der Linkskurven-Sender hatte sich in den vergangenen Jahren der Uefa Champions League Übertragung gesichert, wurde jedoch von Beobachtern immer wieder kritisiert, dass hier in ein sinnloses Wettrüsten eingestiegen wird. Während eine Übertragung des Europa League Spiels unter Umständen nur zwischen 1 bis 1,5 Millionen Euro kosten könnte, sprechen wir bei der Champions-League Übertragung von näherungsweise rund 3,5 Millionen Euro. Das ist ein Intervall, das sich bei einem Sender wie dem ORF oder auch bei vergleichbaren Sendern sehr schwer zu finanzieren ist.
Nun wird jedoch eine gute Nachricht verkündet. Der ORF scheint sich als einziger Kanal in Österreich nicht an einer überhöhten Preisgestaltung beteiligt zu haben. Wie aus Insiderkreisen bekannt wurde, hat der ORF etwa 16 Millionen Euro pro Saison gezahlt, was durchaus günstig erscheinen mag. Im Vergleich dazu, soll sich Sky Österreich an einem Betrag von rund 24 Millionen pro Saison beteiligt haben. Das ist ein sehr deutlicher und überhöhter Unterschied, wenn man in Betracht zieht, dass beide Sender in Österreichs heimischem Markt tätig sind. Dies bedeutet zum Beispiel, dass der ORF deutlich mehr Gewinne abziehen kann als Sky oder andere Kanäle in Österreich.
Ein Grund für die günstige Preisgestaltung beim ORF könnte auch ein spezielles österreichisches Ordnungsgesetz sein. Laut diesem Gesetz dürfen Medienerzeugnisse während einer Übertragung keine eigene Werbung schalten und keine der Sendung eigenen Werbung, die über maximal 60 Sekunden hinaus geht. Andere Kanäle haben diese Verbote jedoch nicht und verwenden dies als eine Art Finanzierungsmöglichkeit. Der ORF muss auf diese Mittel jedoch verzichten und muss stattdessen auf die Kürzung des Kaufpreises setzen.
Zusammenfassend lässt sich sagen, dass die relativ günstige Preise für den ORF zumindest auch eine sehr gute Nachricht für die Konsumenten darstellen. Der Sender kann nun weiterhin die Champions League übertragen und wird das auch in Zukunft tun. Im Vergleich zu anderen Kanälen, die ein vielfaches des Preises gezahlt haben, war es für den ORF offensichtlich die richtige Entscheidung, hier nicht um jeden Preis mitzubieten. Obwohl der Sender in Österreich äußerst populär ist, zeigt dieser Schritt einmal mehr, dass es sowohl bei der Preisgestaltung als auch bei den Inhalten auch um ein gewisses Maß an Vernunft und Besinnung geht.
* die Übertragungsrechte - broadcast rights
* das Wettrüsten - arms race
* die Preisgestaltung - pricing
* das Ordnungsgesetz - regulatory law
* die Kürzung - reduction
* die Vernunft - reason, sense

Roma - Caos, imbarazzi e il timore di una figuraccia finale di proporzioni bibliche. In grado di trascinare giù, insieme al sindaco resistente Ignazio Marino, tutto il Partito democratico. Quello cittadino di sicuro, quello nazionale forse. La rinuncia del sindaco di Roma alle dimissioni, per quanto prevedibile, ha fatto esplodere più di una contraddizione tra i democratici. La strategia decisa dal commissario cittadino Matteo Orfini è rimasta la stessa dei giorni scorsi. Dimissioni in massa dei consiglieri comunali, in modo da sciogliere il consiglio e fare automaticamente decadere il primo cittadino. La moral suasion di Orfini è culminata ieri in un incontro con i consiglieri alla sede nazionale del Pd a Largo del Nazareno. Un modo per dire ai democratici romani che le dimissioni sono un ordine più che una richiesta. Ragioni di forza maggiore e input che arriva direttamente dal premier e segretario del partito Matteo Renzi. Per tutta la giornata di ieri né Orfini né altri erano riusciti a sciogliere il principale nodo. I 19 consiglieri Pd non bastano. Per fare chiudere la legislatura del consiglio comunale romano ne servono 25

Mandating community service for high school students is a topic of much debate. On one hand, it can provide students with a valuable opportunity to give back to their communities and develop important life skills. On the other hand, it may infringe upon their personal time and academic workload. After considering both sides of the argument, it is clear that mandating community service can have significant benefits for high school students.

One major advantage of requiring community service is the opportunity for students to engage with their communities and develop a sense of social responsibility. By volunteering their time and skills, students can develop empathy, compassion, and a sense of purpose. They can gain a greater understanding of the challenges faced by others and develop a desire to work towards positive change. This hands-on experience can be invaluable for their personal growth and development.

Moreover, community service can provide practical experiences that enhance academic learning. Whether it be through mentoring younger students, working with local organizations, or participating in environmental conservation projects, students can gain real-world knowledge and skills that complement their classroom education. These practical experiences can deepen their understanding of academic concepts, foster critical thinking skills, and provide a context for applying knowledge learned in school.

However, it is important to consider the potential drawbacks of mandating community service. High school students already have numerous commitments, including extracurricular activities, part-time jobs, and academic responsibilities. Requiring community service on top of these commitments may result in increased stress levels and a lack of personal time. This can potentially lead to burnout or decreased academic performance.

In conclusion, while mandating community service for high school students may pose challenges in terms of personal time and academic workload, the benefits far outweigh the drawbacks. The opportunity to engage with their communities, develop important life skills, and enhance academic learning is invaluable. Moreover, with proper planning and support, the potential negative impacts on personal time and academic workload can be minimized. Therefore, mandating community service can have significant positive impacts on high school students and should be encouraged.

I've never had to complete a summer project before, but I know some schools do. The students may not be too thrilled about it, but these projects help them be more engaged and continue learning while they are on summer break. There are different ways that school can help make these projects at least a little bit more bearable for the students, like letting them design it themselves. Some people may believe that summer projects should be teacher-designed, gives them more creative freedom, makes the project more enjoyable, teaches students new things even when they aren't at school. Whenever given very specific instructions with little to no room for creativity and individuality, it makes it very difficult for me to get through the project. I know that most students love to put their personality into their work, and when that option is stripped from them it becomes a lot less interesting to complete. When teachers let kids express themselves through their work, more often than not, the results that come out of it are much better than if they weren't. I hate doing projects most of the time because I'm always just given a topic and requirements and am let go to just do whatever work the teacher gave me. I've always found it much easier to do projects when we are allowed to choose what we do them about things that we truly enjoy learning about. By assigning a summer project, students have the opportunity to continue learning even when school is out. When teachers let the students decide for themselves exactly what the project should include, they are going to have a lot more fun with it; in fact, it may actually help them complete the project and impact their studies even farther. When given the opportunity to research something that they are truly interested in and passionate about, the outcome quality would be truly wonderful. however, student-based projects are a better choice because it gives more creative freedom to the students, the project itself becomes more enjoyable, and helps students continue learning even when out of school. Kids love being able to do things they are passionate about, and letting them help with assigning their own projects gives them the opportunity for greatness.

Die Steuerreform tritt zwar erst Anfang 2016 in Kraft, sie hat aber bereits in den ersten Monaten des aktuellen Jahres Spuren hinterlassen. Die Einnahmen aus der Kapitalertragsteuer (KESt) auf Dividenden lagen im ersten Halbjahr nämlich weit über Plan, wie aktuelle Zahlen des  Finanzministeriums zeigen. Konkret wurden 1,16 Milliarden Euro an den Fiskus überwiesen, was einem Anstieg um 26 Prozent gegenüber dem Vorjahr entspricht. Viele Firmen  haben also offenbar Dividendenausschüttungen vorgezogen, weil sie befürchteten, die erhöhte KESt (künftig sind es 27,5 statt 25 Prozent)  könnte bereits während des Jahres eingeführt werden. Zinsen weiter niedrig Auch insgesamt sieht der Budgetvollzug des ersten Halbjahres nicht  schlecht aus. Die gesamten Einnahmen lagen bei 38,19 Milliarden Euro und somit um 4,6 Prozent über dem Vorjahr. Für das Gesamtjahr hat  Finanzminister Hans Jörg Schelling (ÖVP) ein Plus von 4,2 Prozent eingeplant.  Auch auf der Ausgabenseite liegt man gut: Zwar führen die hohen Zahlen  an Arbeitslosen und Asylwerbern zu nicht geplanten Mehrbelastungen, die  Zinszahlungen liegen aber weit unter Plan. Obwohl der Minister also eigentlich nur auf Kurs ist, wurde seine Position  gegenüber Brüssel gestärkt. Wie das möglich ist? An die EU-Kommission wurde eine deutlich niedrigere  Steuerschätzung geschickt. Konkret wurden die Einnahmen im  Strategiebericht der Regierung, der jedes Jahr im April versendet wird,  um 1,5 Milliarden Euro niedriger angesetzt als im Budgetvoranschlag.  Erreicht man nun also die ursprünglichen Einnahmenziele, dürfte es  leichter fallen, das nach Brüssel gemeldete Ziel eines strukturellen Defizits von nur 0,5 Prozent im aktuellen Jahr zu erreichen. Der grüne Budgetsprecher Bruno Rossmann will nun von Schelling wissen,  warum die Steuerschätzung im Strategiebericht derart vorsichtig  ausgefallen ist. Er bringe den Angaben immer weniger Vertrauen entgegen. Daher fordere er eine Offenlegung der Annahmen für die  Schätzung der wichtigsten Steuern. Das kann und darf kein Geheimnis sein. In anderen Ländern hätten die Parlamentarier einen wesentlich  besseren Einblick.

Un cardiologo è stato arrestato con l'accusa di aver abusato sessualmente di alcuni dei suoi pazienti. L'uomo, che non può essere identificato per motivi legali, ha affrontato diverse accuse tra cui molestie e aggressione sessuale a seguito delle indagini della polizia. L'inchiesta è stata avviata dopo che una donna ha presentato un reclamo alla polizia riguardo al comportamento dell'uomo durante la sua visita presso il suo studio medico. La donna ha affermato che l'uomo le aveva fatto domande inappropriate e si era tolto i vestiti davanti a lei senza alcuna ragione apparente. In risposta alle sue proteste, l'uomo sarebbe diventato arrabbiato e minaccioso prima di cacciare via la donna dal suo ufficio. La polizia ha condotto ulteriori interrogatori e ha scoperto che altre due donne avevano subito esperienze simili con lo stesso cardiologo. Entrambe hanno raccontato storie di come fossero state costrette ad eseguire attività intime mentre erano sotto la cura del dottore. Le vittime hanno anche detto agli investigatori che il cardiologo avrebbe usato la sua posizione professionale per manipolare e controllare loro. In base ai risultati dell'indagine, il cardiologo è stato immediatamente sospeso dall'esercizio della medicina ed è ora facendo fronte all'imminenza di un processo penale

Ghiottoni di tutto il mondo unitevi. E marciate verso Brescia, da domani centro di un evento del vino che chiama in potenza altre occasioni per incantare gola e palato. È la terza edizione del Festival del Vino Bresciano, rassegna orchestrata da Centro Vitivinicolo Provinciale e Musei Mazzucchelli in questultima sede, museimazzucchelli.it, a Ciliverghe di Mazzano, una manciata di chilometri a est del capoluogo. Sabato e domenica, dalle 14 alle 21, 5 euro a ingresso, decine di produttori eno-agroalimentari esibiranno largenteria golosa per dimostrare che il territorio sa gettare benissimo lo sguardo oltre la Franciacorta. La botte bresciana contiene infatti uve altre da quegli chardonnay, pinot bianco e pinot nero che marchiano il disciplinare docg di bollicine come quelle dei Barboglio de Gaioncelli di Corte Franca, barbogliodegaioncelli.it, presenti al banco assaggi del museo. Colpisce sempre di più, ad esempio, limpegno delle aziende a ridosso del Garda, i maestri della Lugana, prima doc registrata in Lombardia. Per tutti valgano i prodotti della Perla del Garda, perladelgarda.it, a Ciliverghe con la voce grossa che fa il suo trebbiano di lugana in purezza Madonna della Scoperta (e non solo)

In other words, if something doesn't look like anything found anywhere else on our planet then it's probably considered an alien.   If we accept this definition as true, there are many things around us which could potentially qualify for being called aliens such as: trees, rocks, mountains, rivers, lakes, etc., all of which have no resemblance whatsoever to any form of life known to exist outside of Earth's biosphere.

It is well accepted that taking advice from others is an integral part of life. From our parents to our peers, teachers, or simply those who have achieved success, it is important to seek advice and take it on board when making decisions. Doing so can lead to success across the many aspects of life, whether that be on a personal or professional level. To demonstrate why, I will explore both historical examples and personal experience. 

In terms of history, an example can be found in Bill Gates. In 1989, he and Paul Allen sought the advice of Warren Buffett, a business magnate and one of the most successful investors in the world. This ultimately enabled them to distinguish between long and short-term investments, ultimately leading to a significant increase in their wealth and success by 1995. Furthermore, taking on board Boltman’s advice to patent their products could also be seen as a key factor in their continued success.

On a more personal level, I experienced this all throughout my studies. From the guidance of my teachers on writing essays to the support of my friends, listening and taking on their advice resulted in success throughout my academic career. 

It is clear that the value of listening to others cannot be overstated. As well as providing technical expertise, it has been demonstrated to increase motivation and boost self-confidence, traits that are key to success. Being open to receiving advice from those around us, whether it be to take an alternate route, or simply that there are no limits to what we can achieve, is something that we must all strive for in order to lead the most successful life possible. 

In conclusion, seeking and listening to the advice of those around us is an important part of life. Going into any venture with a plan and understanding our surroundings, alongside any technical knowledge we have, puts us in a strong position. Ultimately, taking advice from others can lead to significant success, both on a professional and personal level. Many of us have used it to improve our lives, and we can learn from those who have gone before us in order to make positive life changes today.

Arbeit macht Freude, ist sinnstiftend und kann ein wichtiger Beitrag zur sozialen Inklusion von Menschen mit Beeinträchtigungen sein. Seit November sind zehn von dem Salzburger Verein Lebenshilfe betreute Menschen bei der Personalvermittlungsfirma Teampool im Flachgauer Seekirchen beschäftigt. Sie sortieren Werbemittel und verpacken diese. Ein ähnliches Modell gibt es bei einem Betrieb für Reinigungstechnik. Der Wermutstropfen dabei: Alle sind nur auf geringfügiger Basis beschäftigt. Würden sie mehr verdienen, würden sie aus der Behindertenbeihilfe des Landes fallen, sagt Vereinssprecherin Claudia Tomasini. Nicht zuletzt diese Konstruktion in dem von 1981 stammenden Salzburger Behindertengesetz führe dazu, dass die meisten der Lebenshilfe-Klienten nur für ein Taschengeld von etwa 100 Euro im Monat arbeiten können. 200 der 740 von der Lebenshilfe Betreuten sind so bei Firmen oder Gemeinden tätig – nur unfallversichert, ohne sonstige sozialversicherungsrechtliche Ansprüche. Dass es auch anders gehe, zeige das Vorarlberger Projekt Spagat, sagt Tomasini. Bei diesem Inklusionsprojekt werden Behinderte angestellt, der Betrieb bezahlt aber nur die tatsächlich erbrachte Leistung. Die Differenz auf das kollektivvertragliche Entgelt – maximal 1.000 Euro monatlich – legt das Land dazu. Insgesamt nehmen in Vorarlberg bereits rund 300 Menschen an dem Arbeits- und Inklusionsprojekt teil. Von solchen Fortschritten können die Salzburger nur träumen. Denn auch in der von Soziallandesrat Heinrich Schellhorn (Grüne) vor einigen Wochen vorgelegten Novelle zum Behindertengesetz seien solche Projekte nicht vorgesehen, kritisiert man bei der Lebenshilfe. Es sei eben in Österreich immer noch eine Frage des Glücks, wo man auf die Welt kommt, so Tomasini. Auch Lebenshilfe-Geschäftsführer Guido Güntert sieht in der Gesetzesnovelle wenige Fortschritte. Generalkritikpunkt aus seiner Sicht: Das zentrale Grundprinzip der UN-Behindertenrechtskonvention – die Selbstbestimmung im Rahmen der Möglichkeiten – spiegelt sich im Landesgesetz in keinster Weise wider. Als Beispiel für die Selbstbestimmung nennt Güntert die Wohnplatzwahl. Auch weiterhin werde Menschen mit Beeinträchtigungen ein Wohnort zugewiesen, Mitbestimmung sei im neuen Gesetz nicht vorgesehen. Ähnlich die Beurteilung durch die gesetzliche Bewohnervertretung des Vereins Vertretungsnetzwerk. Die Novelle enthalte vor allem Änderungen in der Terminologie, es gebe keinen Paradigmenwechsel und keine Verbesserungen für die betroffenen Menschen, sagt die stellvertretende Bereichsleiterin für Salzburg und Tirol, Alexandra Niedermoser. Der von Schellhorn – er war urlaubsbedingt für eine Stellungnahme nicht erreichbar – als Kernstück bezeichnete Inklusionsbeirat, in dem auch Betroffene sitzen, ist für Niedermoser wenig glaubwürdig. Dieser habe keine durchgreifenden Kompetenzen, und seine Unabhängigkeit ist aufgrund der organisatorischen Nähe zum Land fraglich. Im Kern dürfte sich der neue Gesetzesinhalt vom Text des Jahres 1981 jedenfalls wenig unterscheiden. Ein Beispiel von vielen: Die Feststellung der Behinderung und der notwendigen Maßnahmen solle weiterhin nur durch die Ärzte des Landes erfolgen. Die Beiziehung anderer Experten wie etwa Sonder- oder Heilpädagogen sei nicht vorgesehen, kritisiert Niedermoser.

Comincia ad affiorare un pezzo di verità sulla tragedia aerea dell' Airbus in Provenza. Il co-pilota dell’A320 caduto nel sud della Francia è rimasto da solo in cabina e ha azionato "volontariamente" la discesa dell’aereo. Lo ha confermato il procuratore di Marsiglia Brice Robin, aggiungedo che il respiro del co-pilota è registrato nella scatola nera fini al momento dell’impatto, il che significa che si è deliberatamente rifiutato di aprire al comandante rimasto fuori. "Ad oggi, alla luce delle indagini in corso, l’interpretazione più plausibile per noi inquirenti è che il copilota abbia volontariamente permesso la perdita di quota dell’aereo e che avesse la volontà di distruggere questo aereo", ha affermato il procuratore. Secondo la timeline ricostruita dal quotidiano, alle 10.30 l’Airbus, identificativo radio " Germanwings One Eight Golf", conferma le istruzioni dalla torre di controllo francese. Alle 10.31.02, tuttavia, il velivolo abbandona l’altitudine assegnata e comincia a scendere: il radar registra una discesa di circa 17,8 metri al secondo. I controllori provano a mettersi in contatto con la cabina di volo senza successo. Alle 10.35

The question of whether all students should be required to participate in extracurricular activities is a complex one, and the answer will depend on a variety of factors. On the one hand, extracurricular activities can provide many benefits to students, teachers, and parents. On the other hand, there are also drawbacks that must be considered when making a decision on this issue. 

From the student’s perspective, extracurricular activities can provide many positive benefits. For example, research suggests that participating in clubs, sports, and other extracurricular activities can help build social skills and promote academic success. Additionally, participation in extracurricular activities helps to develop leadership skills and self-confidence, and can provide valuable networking and work experience. With these advantages, students stand to gain a lot from being involved in extracurricular activities.

For teachers, requiring all students to participate in extracurricular activities can help them to better understand their students. This can be useful for teachers in developing creative strategies to engage their students and improve their educational experiences. Additionally, some extracurricular activities can provide a means of teaching important life skills, such as teamwork and problem solving. This allows teachers to help their students become better prepared for the future.

For parents, extracurricular activities can provide a sense of relief, as there is an increased sense of structure and responsibility for their children. Furthermore, it can provide an opportunity for parents and children to enjoy activities together, which can be a great bonding experience. Extracurricular activities can also ensure that children are finding a healthy balance between school and their social and recreational lives.

At the same time, there are also drawbacks to requiring all students to participate in extracurricular activities. For many students, the added commitment of extracurricular activities could lead to increased stress and anxiety. Additionally, forcing students into activities they may not enjoy or have the time for can be a disincentive for continued involvement, as students may not feel that their needs are being met. Finally, depending on the specific activities chosen, the cost to parents of having their children participate could be significant. 

Overall, while extracurricular activities can provide many benefits to students, teachers, and parents, there is also potential for drawbacks. Ultimately, the decision to require all students to take part in extracurricular activities should depend on the resources available and the needs of the individual school or student body. 

Consideration must also be taken regarding the type of activities chosen, as there can be both physical and financial costs involved. With these points in mind, it is possible to make an informed decision on whether all students should be required to participate in extracurricular activities. 

In conclusion, the decision to require all students to be involved in extracurricular activities should be made with careful consideration of the benefits and drawbacks. This includes taking into account the resources available, the interests of the students, and any costs associated with the activity. This information should then be used to make a decision that best suits the needs of the students, teachers, and parents.

Stefan Fegerl, der österreichische Tischtennisspieler, ist der große Sieger der Tischtennis-Europameisterschaften. Der 27-Jährige sicherte sich am Wochenende gleich zwei Goldmedaillen: Eine im Einzel und eine im Doppel, den er mit dem Schweden Kristian Karlsson gewann. Fegerl, der Nummer Zwei des österreichischen Teams, konnte im Einzel in einem beeindruckenden Finale den Spanier Ovidiu Ionescu besiegen. Im Doppel hingegen war der Erfolg im Grunde genommen unausweichlich: Fegerl und Karlsson hatten eine beeindruckende Bilanz aufzuweisen und wurden im Vorfeld als einer der Top-Favoriten gehandelt.
Die beiden Goldmedaillen sind ein großer Erfolg für den Österreicher und setzen gleichzeitig ein Ausrufezeichen für die österreichische Tischtennis-Mannschaft. Fegerl, der auch in der Deutschen Bundesliga spielt, galt bereits im Vorfeld der Euro als aussichtsreicher Kandidat auf eine Einzelmedaille. Dass es am Ende sogar für den Titel reichte, zeigt, dass er momentan zu den besten Spielern in Europa gehört.
Erfolge wie diese sorgen auch dafür, dass der Tischtennissport in Österreich etwas mehr in den Fokus rückt. Immerhin konnte die Mannschaft bei den Europameisterschaften zwei Medaillen sammeln und fungieren auch in den internationalen Ranglisten immer mehr als ernsthafte Konkurrenz. Auch für den Tischtennis-Sport insgesamt sind Erfolge wie dieser wichtig, um mehr Zuschauer zu animieren und mehr Unterstützer zu finden.
Für Stefan Fegerl selbst sind diese beiden Medaillen allerdings gelebte Erfolge und zeigen, dass harte Arbeit und Disziplin im Sport sich lohnen können. Der Österreicher trainiert hart bei seinem Verein Werder Bremen und arbeitet akribisch an seiner Technik und seinen Fertigkeiten. Dass diese Disziplin nun belohnt wurde, ist ein großer Triumph für den Sportler und auch für das Trainerteam, das mit ihm arbeitet.
Insgesamt können wir festhalten, dass die Tischtennis-Europameisterschaften ein großer Erfolg waren. Es wurde guter Sport geboten und es gab viele herausragende Leistungen zu sehen. Besonders freuen wir uns darüber, dass ein Österreicher das Turnier so erfolgreich für sich gestalten konnte. Wir gratulieren Stefan Fegerl herzlich zu diesen beiden Goldmedaillen und freuen uns auf weitere spannende Wettkämpfe in diesem faszinierenden Sport.  Insgesamt

Il presidente siriano Bashar al-Assad ha recentemente fatto la voce grossa, affermando che il suo paese è pronto a intraprendere rappresaglie in risposta alle accuse di aver utilizzato armi chimiche contro i propri cittadini. In un'intervista alla Cbs News, Assad ha negato l'uso di armi chimiche e ha detto: "Non c'è prova". L'accusa di utilizzo di armi chimiche da parte del governo siriano non è nuova; le Nazioni Unite hanno condannato ripetutamente gli attacchi con armi chimiche nel corso della guerra civile siriana, iniziata nell'aprile 2011. Gli Stati Uniti sono stati particolarmente critici dell'utilizzo delle armi chimiche dal momento dello scoppio dei conflitti. Nel settembre 2013, gli Stati Uniti avevano minacciato di lanciare una serie di attacchi missilistici su obiettivi militari siriani se il governo avesse continuato ad usare tali armi. Tuttavia, dopo lunghe trattative diplomatiche tra gli Stati Uniti, la Russia e altri stakeholder internazionali, fu raggiunto un accordo per eliminare completamente lo stock di armi chimiche dalla Siria entro la fine del 2014. Nonostante questo accordo, ci furono segnalazioni di ulteriori attacchi con armi chimiche nella primavera del 2017, portando gli Stati Uniti a bombardare una base militare siriana come rappresaglia

When someone hears the words "driverless cars", they get excited at the thought of something so futuristic at one time that it seems like something out of a science fiction novel. However, now that dream is becoming a reality. Large companies such as Google or BMW are advancing in this highly experimental field, and Google has been since as early as 2009  But is letting the machine take over most of the time really the best option? Could it lead to even more problems than it hopes to fix? These questions have yet to be answered and need to be. 
 In 2013, BMW began developing a project they dubbed the "Traffic Jam Assistant", allowing a car to handle all possible functions up to the speed of 25 mph. But even though the car drove itself, the driver still had to remain alert and keep both hands on the wheel where sensors were located. And in such situations as work zones or accidents, the driver had to take over.  Moments like these should always be handled by the driver, because the only thing a vehicle needs is one single driver  If a pedestrian ever makes the mistake of crossing too late on a crosswalk in the middle of the street, the car is not going to be ablle to know if it is coming or not or whether the car can steer clear of causing an accident because of human error.  There is also the consideration of laws. In our current era, whoever hits someone initially in a traffic collision, is the one to blame, and the one who helps pay off the other driver's bill. Human error is a natural thing, and we all make mistakes, but if there is ever an accident in which an autonomous car is made and is involved in an accident, who is responsible in the end? Is it the driver, or is it the car which is supposed to be incapable of making mistakes?  Allowing a car to be responsible for one person's life is not the best decision, and should be thought out more accordingly, than just seeing it as a fun toy because our laziness is growing so much, that we can't even drive for ourselves.  Then there is the cost, both on our wealth and our experience. If we allow cars to drive us around and take us anywhere we like, what will happen when it's our turn to drive. We will be less alert, paying attention to our surroundings instead of focusing on the road.  Some may argue that cars come with warning signals that the driver must take control soon  but will they have enough time to completely survey the area and assess the situation? Or will the car be able to warn them in time? The answer is most likely to be answered, but is still a dangerous situation for a new driver.  In 20 years these cars may be a new one of society's norms, and everyone might own a car that drives on its own. But before we can reach that day, we have to decide if we're willing to pay large amounts of dollars for a car that takes away all feeling of something belonging to us and seeming to be in our control. The "Cars of the Future" will cost extraneous amounts of dollars, just for the ability for us to drive only some of the time, and not all of it. 
 Some people may argue that there are beneficial sides to an autonomous car, and of course there are. These cars would cut down our costs on gas, they could even be safer than some humans driving cars, and they could all be traced around on a track designed just for the cars.  But it all comes back down to cost. The thought of replacing all roads with "Smart Roads" is not only seemingly impossible, but timewise it would take a lifetime. If every single road was taken apart and rebuilt all over America, the world even, it would take at least 10 years, and the cost would be enormous. And these cars are safe, but if that situation ever hits, no one is going to want their death or disability caused by a car driving on its own because of human error, yet again.  The concept is definitely real, and they have continued to being developed. The only thing to see now is if they can pay the price, develop more solutions than problems, and see if laws can be passed in their favor. It will take years, or maybe even a decade before we see who will be driving behind the wheel in the end, the human, or the car itself.

The Electoral College system in the United States has long been a subject of much debate and controversy. On one hand, proponents argue that it ensures balance and protects smaller states from being overshadowed by larger ones. On the other hand, critics argue that it can lead to a discrepancy between the popular vote and the final election outcome, ultimately undermining the democratic principles upon which the country is founded. In my opinion, the disadvantages of the Electoral College system outweigh its advantages, and it is time for a change.

One of the main advantages often cited in support of the Electoral College system is that it prevents larger, more populous states from dominating the election and marginalizing smaller states. The system achieves this by allocating a certain number of electors to each state based on its population, giving smaller states a proportionally larger representation. This ensures that the concerns and interests of all states, regardless of size, are taken into account during the election process.

However, this advantage comes at a significant cost. The Electoral College system can result in a situation where the candidate who wins the majority of the popular vote does not necessarily win the election. This discrepancy has occurred several times in American history, including the 2000 and 2016 presidential elections. Such outcomes undermine the democratic principles of "one person, one vote" and can lead to a lack of faith in the electoral process. In an era where transparency and trust in democratic institutions are paramount, it is essential that the voice of the people is accurately reflected in the election results.

Furthermore, the Electoral College system can also lead to the concentration of campaign efforts in battleground states, effectively ignoring the concerns and interests of citizens in non-swing states. This prioritization of a select few states diminishes the importance of every citizen's vote and perpetuates a sense of inequality among voters. Every American citizen, regardless of their place of residence, should have an equal say in the outcome of the election.

In conclusion, while the Electoral College system may have some advantages in terms of state representation, its disadvantages far outweigh these benefits. The discrepancy between the popular vote and the final outcome undermines democratic principles and fosters a lack of trust in the electoral process. It is time for the United States to reevaluate its electoral system and consider alternatives that prioritize the voice and will of the people.

Driverless cars could positively impact the way people drive by reducing the number of automobile accidents on the road, thereby saving lives. Additionally, by enabling the cars to drive themselves, people won't have to worry about driving while distracted by tasks such as dialing phone numbers or eating and therefore, potentially enhancing productivity. Additionally, by dramatically cutting down on pollution from vehicles, driverless cars have the potential to improve the air quality in cities worldwide. Despite these appealing qualities, there are several concerns regarding driverless cars that could potentially limit the impact they could have on society. For example, because current driverless car technology is unable to distinguish between legitimate road signs and certain hazards such as speed cameras, the cars might fail to detect an obstacle in the road and cause an accident. Additionally, there are concerns that hackers could potentially access car computers and intentionally cause accidents, for example, to harm the driverless car industry. Despite these concerns, it is likely that driverless cars will be adopted in the coming years, leading to increased safety and productivity for the average driver. Overall, by reducing the risks associated with automobile accidents and by increasing driver productivity, driverless cars have the potential to positively impact society in profound ways. While driverless cars could have a profoundly positive impact on society, they will need to overcome significant challenges before becoming a ubiquitous part of everyday life. One of the biggest challenges they will need to overcome is ensuring the safety of the technology, that hackers cannot harm the technology and that the hardware is able to withstand the rigors of driving. Another important factor to consider is user adoption, namely for consumers to embrace the idea of not having to drive and rely on driverless cars. While driverless cars could increase productivity, theisure industry must also find ways to integrate this technology and make it a selling point. Ultimately, while there are some concerns regarding driverless cars, they have the potential to positively impact society in ways that are similar to how horse carriages changed transportation - incrementally improving safety and efficiency while giving the public more options to choose from. Overall, while there are some concerns regarding driverless cars, they have the potential to positively impact society. One of the most profound impacts they could have is reducing traffic accidents and traffic jams by allowing cars to drive themselves. Furthermore, they have the potential to dramatically increase productivity and reduce environmental pollution. Finally, there is a huge market opportunity to deliver driverless cars to the general public. One of the challenges so far has been that the technology needed to drive cars has been very expensive. However, recent investments in artificial intelligence and other technologies have the potential to bring down the cost of driverless cars significantly. In conclusion, while there are some concerns regarding driverless cars, they have the potential to positively impact society. References: [1] https://www.wired.com/2017/09/driverless-cars-are-coming-save-lives-cut-accidents/ [2] https://www.technologyreview.com/s/202296/driverless-car-the-great-debate-continues/ [3] https://www.technologyreview.com/s/6023v/what-will-happen-to-car-insurance-once-driverless-cars-appear/

In meno di un anno, oltre 40mila immigrati sono arrivati in Italia. Questo numero è cresciuto significativamente rispetto agli anni precedenti e sta mettendo a dura prova le risorse del paese. La maggior parte degli immigrati provengono dall'Africa subsahariana e dal Medio Oriente, con molti che cercano rifugio dalle guerre civili o dalla povertà economica. Gli effetti dell'afflusso massiccio di migranti possono essere visti ovunque nel paese. Le città costiere come Lampedusa e Catania stanno affrontando una mancanza di alloggi adeguato per gli immigrati appena arrivati. Ciò ha causato sovraffollamento nei campi profughi esistenti, portando ad insalubrità ed epidemie di malattia. Anche i servizi sanitari pubblici sono stati messi sotto pressione mentre si sforzano di fornire assistenza medica ai nuovi arrivati. Il governo italiano sta lottando per gestire la situazione. Ha implementato politiche più restrittive riguardo all'ingresso dei richiedenti asilo, ma ciò non sembra aver avuto alcun impatto sulla quantità di persone che continuano ad entrare nel paese ogni giorno. Il governo sta anche collaborando con organizzazioni internazionali come il Consiglio d'Europa e le Nazioni Unite per trovare soluzioni alla crisi umanitaria

Tra meno di un mese Raniero Busco saprà se per la giustizia italiana è stato lui, il 7 agosto del 1990, ad uccidere Simonetta Cesaroni, la giovane impiegata massacrata in un ufficio di via Poma, a Roma. All'epoca Busco, addetto ai servizi a terra dell'aeroporto di Fiumicino, era fidanzato con Simonetta, ma inizialmente non finì tra i sospettati, come ha ricordato ieri davanti alla Corte d'Assise, nell'ultima udienza prima della conclusione della fase istruttoria, il giornalista radiofonico Giampiero Marzi. «In questura, durante la conferenza stampa, dissero che Busco era estraneo alla morte della ragazza in quanto quando avvenne il fatto lui lavorava», ha rivelato il cronista. Nessuno all'epoca pensò di mettere nero su bianco l'alibi del ragazzo che ora, a distanza di 20 anni, si ritrova sul banco degli imputati a dover rendere conto di quanto accaduto allora. Ora il dibattimento è finito. Le prossime udienze sono state già fissate per la requisitoria del pm Ilaria Calò e per le arringhe dei difensori. Il 20 gennaio (o al più tardi il 26), dopo le repliche, la Corte dovrebbe entrare in camera di consiglio

Critics of the Electoral College argue that it is unfair, outdated, and irrational in the way that it selects the President of the United States. Despite these criticisms, there are still many who believe that the Electoral College should not be abolished. The primary reasons for this belief are the intent of the Founding Fathers, the regional diversity of America, the safeguard against political corruption, the restriction of power from special interests, and the promotion of the popular vote. 

The Electoral College was created in the United States Constitution by the Founding Fathers in order to balance power between the bigger and smaller states. According to the principle of Federalism, the Founding Fathers wanted to protect the smaller states from larger states from having immense power over them. The Electoral College ensures each state an equal number of electors in the House of Representatives, which allows smaller states to have a powerful influence on the election. 

In addition, the regional diversity of the United States is another reason why the Electoral College should remain intact. Different states and regions in the country have different political opinions. By virtue of the Electoral College, the candidates can see the different views and opinions of the people across the nation, allowing for a more balanced presidential decision-making process.

The Electoral College also serves as a safeguard against political corruption and unfairness in elections. Since the Electoral College is composed of members from each state, it helps to protect the voting process from any kind of external pressure or manipulation. This creates a situation in which the president is elected based on the popular vote alone and ensures that the voice of the people is heard.

Furthermore, the continued existence of the Electoral College helps to restrict the power of special interests and individuals in elections. In correlation with the aforementioned explanation, special interests cannot change the outcome of an election by pooling a substantial amount of money into one particular candidate.

Finally, the Electoral College promotes the will of the people by involving them in the presidential decision-making process. The popular vote, or the vote of the people, is actually the deciding factor in a presidential election. Votes from a majority of states’ electors will ultimately decide who will become the President of the United States.

In conclusion, although the Electoral College has been criticized by some, there are five good reasons why it should not be abolished. These reasons include the intent of the Founding Fathers, the regional diversity of America, the safeguard against political corruption, the restriction of power from special interests, and the promotion of the popular vote. Each of these reasons make a strong case for the continued use of the Electoral College in the United States.

La Juventus ha appena annunciato un'importante operazione di mercato che coinvolge due dei suoi giocatori. Medhi Benatia, il difensore marocchino della squadra bianconera, si trasferirà in Qatar per vestire la maglia dell'Al-Duhail SC, mentre Martin Caceres tornerà a Torino dopo aver trascorso l'anno scorso al Lazio. Benatia è arrivato alla Vecchia Signora nel 2016 e da allora è diventato una presenza fissa nella formazione titolare del club italiano. È stato uno strumento chiave nelle vittorie dello Scudetto degli ultimi tre anni ed è considerato uno dei migliori centrali difensivi del calcio europeo. Il suo nuovo contratto con l'Al-Duhail lo renderà anche uno dei calciatori più pagati al mondo. Caceres, d'altra parte, aveva lasciato la Juventus nell'estate del 2018 per firmare con i rivali italiani del Lazio. Tuttavia, nonostante le sue buone prestazioni durante la sua stagione a Roma, non è riuscito ad imporsi come titolare fisso e ora fa ritorno a Torino dove spera di ottenere maggior tempo di gioco sotto la guida dell'allenatore Maurizio Sarri

Milano - Crocefisso in aula no, crocefisso in aula sì. Su Facebook impazzano le reazioni alla decisione della Corte europea di Strasburgo che ha bocciato la presenza dei crocefissi nelle classi. Il più ricco di messaggi è il gruppo "Sì al crocifisso nelle scuole" che a metà giornata già contava oltre 14 mila membri e a seguire "Tu stacchi il crocefisso dal muro? Il governo greco annuncia che non ripianerà il suo debito con il Fondo Monetario internazionale. L'ennesima puntata del braccio di ferro tra Atene e resto d'Europa minaccia la riapertura dei mercati di oggi. Che, fino a ora, non hanno però dato serio credito alle due minacce che continuano a incombere: un default sui titoli greci e soprattutto l'uscita del Paese dall'euro. Forse anche perché già nel 2012 Atene ha rifilato agli investitori un «haircut» del 50%, che non ha poi sconvolto più di tanto i listini. Vedremo oggi se si tratterà solo di un occasione per entrare in un mercato che resta rialzista grazie al Qe della Bce, oppure qualcosa di più. Di certo sono in molti, in Europa, a considerare gli annunci del governo Tsipras come tentativi di tenere sotto pressione i falchi che, in Germania come altrove, restano rigidi nel non concedere deroghe ai piani di austerità previsti per i greci. Ora sembra che il governo di Atene non sarà in grado di pagare le rate del prestito del Fmi entro il 5 giugno perchè non ha i soldi per farlo. «Le quattro rate per l'Fmi - ha detto in tv il ministro dell'Interno Nikos Voutsis - a giugno ammontano a 1,6 miliardi. Questo denaro non sarà versato, perché non c'è»

Seeking advice from multiple sources in different situations is a wise practice, which helps individuals make better decisions and cope with life challenges. One such example where this practice helped an individual make a better decision was in a situation involving making a career decision. As an example, one could cite the case of Shane Dawson, a popular YouTube vlogger. In one of his videos, Shane Dawson mentioned that he had once decided to quit his job to pursue a career in video making, but later decided to continue working at his current job. In this example, Shane Dawson sought advice from friends, family, and even tried out his newfound vlogging skills for a while before making a final decision to return to his current job. It can be inferred from this example that it is wise for individuals to seek advice from multiple sources when making major decisions in their lives. Reich also mentions in his book "How to Change Work: challenge assumptions that guide your life" that seeking advice from multiple sources is essential for career change. He provides the example of Larry Page and his experience switching careers from engineering to Google, which was career-changing for many reasons. Larry Page consulted with many experts on career change, and also received input from his close friends, and family. Larry Page's example illustrates the importance of seeking advice from multiple sources and the need to filter through this advice to arrive at a final decision. Reich's book also provides an example of an individual who did not seek advice from multiple sources, who ended up making a bad decision. The example involves an individual who was deciding on purchasing a car, and immediately went to a car dealership and asked for suggestions on what kind of a car to buy. This example shows that individuals sometimes fail to gather adequate information or sift through the correct information to arrive at a decision. This failure to seek advice from multiple sources in different situations leads to poor decision-making and as a result, can greatly affect the lives of individuals.

Exploring Venus can be incredibly dangerous, yet there is tremendous potential for science and human understanding if successful. The challenges and dangers associated with exploring Venus are great, but the potential benefits could outweigh the costs. 

The extreme temperatures, gravity, and atmospheric pressure of Venus make it a near-impossible environment for human exploration. Venus’ average surface temperature of 460 degrees Celsius, its atmospheric pressure of 92 bars—which could crush a human—and its gravity makes it almost impossible for humans to ever set foot on the planet. In addition, the strong ultraviolet radiation of the sun in the planet’s upper atmosphere makes it difficult for missions to survive long enough to gather valuable data.

Despite these immense challenges, if we could overcome them, the potentially transformative benefits of studying Venus can be enormous. Scientists believe that if they can make their way to the lower levels of the planet’s atmosphere, they could find evidence of microbial life. This could add to our understanding of how life on Earth formed and provide clues about whether life could exist elsewhere in the universe. In addition, analyzing the physical properties of Venus could inform research into global warming on Earth. 

Venus exploration technologies could also help us to create more efficient spacecrafts that could make future inter-planetary travel safer and more reliable. Advanced robotics technologies could create much more efficient methods for gathering data than the current standard. These advances are crucial if we are to take on a much more ambitious exploration series than Mars.

Studying Venus is incredibly dangerous, but the potential benefits could be immense. A successful mission could help us understand the origins of life as well as how global warming works. It could also develop technology that could reduce the risks of future interplanetary travel and open up countless new discoveries. If we can overcome the challenges and risks associated with exploring Venus, we could make tremendous advances in science and discovery. . 

In conclusion, the challenges of exploring Venus may be daunting, but the potential benefits of studying the planet can be extraordinarily beneficial to humankind. It is well worth the risk.

Extracurricular activities can be a number of things. From sports, to working on the yearbook, and even serving on the student council, there are a lot of things that qualify as extracurricular activities. It is good for everyone to be part of at least one activity to keep them occupied and stay involved with the school. Doing an extracurricular activity has a lot of benefits, and doing an extracurricular activity is doing yourself a favor.  Whether it gives you good experience for your future career, helps you develop good leadership and decision-making, or keeps you in shape, the pros of requiring extracurricular activities far outweighs the cons.  One of the extracurricular activities you can choose is playing a sport. Sports help you out in a number of ways. They could help you stay in shape. You can make friends or develop stronger bonds with them. They also can be a lot of fun, and pave way for a future career in that sport. There are lots of sports to choose from, so the options are endless!  Another way to stay involved with your school is to work on the yearbook. The yearbook is made annually by students, and is good to put on your resume if you want a career in photography. Helping out with the yearbook actually helps the school too. If students like the yearbook, they will buy it, which gives the school more money to spend on improving itself. You also get to hang out with friends while working on the yearbook, which is always nice.  If those activities dont appeal to you, you could serve on the student council  Serving on the school council helps develop crucial skills like leadership and decision-making. It is also a very good experience if you want to pursue a career in politics later on in life. The decisions you make while on student council can help out the school a lot. You can improve key areas of the school and make life better for the students who go to the school.  Learning new things about yourself is always good, and extracurricular activities help you do just that. Maybe you're a great hockey player. Maybe you discover a passion for photography you never knew you had by working on the yearbook. Maybe you're actually a great leader. There are so many things you can discover about yourself by participating in extracurricular activities. Things that you would never know about if you didn't try an extracurricular activity.

The debate continues today as scientists continue their research trying to find out exactly what's going on at the site known only as "The Face On Mars".

He says he's seen hundreds upon hundreds of photos taken by space shuttles orbiting around Earth looking down towards our neighboring planet but never once did they see anything resembling "the face on mars."   In fact, according to him,  all these pictures show is a large rocky mountain called "MARATHON MOUNTAIN" which happens to look very much like "a man standing up straight facing forward."

The Electoral College is broken, and with another election on the way, here is why the voting should be changed to popular vote for the president of the United States. Voters cannot control whom their electors are, in the chance that those electors are replaced with new electors so the votes may be rigged "faithless" electors may refuse to vote for their party's candidate and could cast a deciding vote for whomever they please.  Voters don't actually vote for the president, instead, they vote for a slate of electors, who in turn elect the president  In the case that you vote John Kerry in Texas, you'd vote for a slate of 34 Democratic electors pledged to Kerry. In the case those electors won the statewide election, they would go to Congress and Kerry would get 34 electoral vote. Who are the electors? They could be anyone, even if their not holding office. Who even picks these electors in the first place? Sometimes it state conventions, other times it's the state's central committee, sometimes the presidential candidates themselves. Can we, citziens of the United States, control whom their electors vote for? Not always, and that shouldn't be.
 In the 1960 election, segregationists in the Louisiana legislature almost succeeded in replacing the Democratic electors with new electors with new electors who would oppose John F. Kennedy and make it seem that a popular vote would have not gone to Kennedy. What would happen if their was a tie in the electoral vote? The case would be thrown in the House of Representatives, where state delegations vote on the president. Becasue each state counts for only one vote, a single representative from Wyoming would represent 500,000 voters, 55 representatives from California who getto represent 55 representatives would have as much say as the one from Wyoming. This cannot represent the will of the people.  Now when have you changed your mind about something? Electors can do the same. Electors can refuse to vote for their party's candidate and vote for whomever they please  That can't be right, yet it has happened plently of times before. It's even unfair to the people, who sometimes don't even get to see their electors. Because of the winner-take-all system in each state, they don't focus of states they know they cannot win, aiming towards "swing states." In the 2000 campaign, 17 states didn't see their candidates at all, as well as 25 of the largest media markets who didn't get to see not one campaign ad.
 The Electoral College dosen't hear the voice of the people, hardly plays by the rules, is something that should be erased. Even people like Richard Nixon, Jimmy Carter, Bob Dale, the U.S. Chamber of Commerce, and the AFL-CIO agree, give the voice back to the people and abolish the Electoral College!

The electoral college is a foundational element of the United States' democracy, serving as a moderating force that fosters presidential candidates' attention to diverse geographic regions and socioeconomic groups. Contrary to the notion of relying solely on popular votes, the electoral college delivers a representative outcome that accounts for the country's diverse demographics and fosters a more balanced form of decision-making.

One of the primary advantages of the electoral college is its inherent stability in maintaining a capable presidency. The system's goal is to prevent any candidate from solely appealing to the masses and instead encourages outreach to wider electorates. This inadvertently ensures that the elected president has a broader mandate to govern, thus fostering economic stability and competent leadership. 

The electoral college system also offers a safety net against impulsive decision-making. Should a candidate solely focus on appealing to the public's immediate desires, the electoral college empowers the electorate to make a more informed decision taking into account the candidate's ability to lead and govern. This measure is especially pertinent in today's social climate, where political polarization often drives impulsive, unrealistic voting trends. 

Moreover, the electoral college facilitates political stability by mitigating the risk of extremist candidates emerging from fringe movements. Without the electoral college, a candidate could potentially capitalize on public sentiment and gain power through sensationalism and extremist ideologies. This scenario could jeopardize the economy by introducing policies that prioritize short-term gains at the expense of long-term stability and growth. 

In conclusion, the electoral college is an essential component of the United States' democracy, promoting balanced decision-making and moderate leadership. It ensures that the president is elected with a broad mandate, safeguarding against impulsive choices and extreme candidates. Ultimately, the electoral college's stability is paramount for sustaining a thriving economy and harmonious society.
